summary: Hunter Seamons' matrix of the 6/22/73 Vancouver show delivers crystal‑clear sound and a soaring Bird Song plus a legendary China Cat > Rider sequence.
review: The matrix recording is hailed as virtually flawless – Hunter’s blend of audience ambience with Charlie Miller’s SBD source gives exceptional clarity, separation and dynamic range.  Musically the concert is described as an “exploratory masterpiece” highlighted by an epic Bird Song, a smoking China Cat Sunflower > Rider, a marathon Here Comes Sunshine and a breathtaking Wharf Rat.  Reviewers are uniformly ecstatic, noting superb interplay among the band members.

ai_rating.rationale: Out of 29 reviews the average rating is ~4.48; 25 reviewers give five stars and praise both performance and sound, with only a few isolated low scores.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hunter Seamons' matrix combines audience ambience with Charlie Miller’s SBD transfer; reviewers repeatedly note crisp separation, full dynamic range, and an almost studio‑like clarity with no significant artifacts.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > The Other One > Rider (China>Rider)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is described as expansive and exploratory, with seamless transitions and a second‑set jam that builds into epic peaks; the setlist’s pacing keeps listeners engaged from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia's guitar tone and lead solo (especially on China Cat Sunflower) are repeatedly praised; his plaintive vocal on Bird Song is highlighted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work is called “spectacular,” with ‘bombs’ that drive the jams; several reviewers note his powerful low‑end throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s lead solo in China Cat Sunflower is singled out as one of his best ever, and his rhythm work is praised.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's piano solo on 'The Race Is On' receives specific commendation; overall keyboard texture is noted as clear and well‑balanced.
